title: Sudan Conflict Update — Mar 22, 2026
summary: Sudan's catastrophic civil war enters day 1072 with two formally declared famines in El-Fasher and Kadugli, and 400,000 people facing starvation. OHCHR published findings in February 2026 concluding RSF violations during the capture of El Fasher constitute war crimes. Fighting intensified in Kordofan in early 2026 with almost daily drone strikes on civilian infrastructure including markets and health facilities. The UN Fact-Finding Mission previously concluded the RSF committed genocide against non-Arab Indigenous groups. Sudan hosts the world's largest humanitarian crisis with 24.6 million food insecure and 12.1 million displaced.
